data.title: Hong Kong Advances Digital Asset Development with Strategic Initiatives
data.description:
Hong Kong's Deputy Secretary for Financial Services and the Treasury, Chen Haolian, highlighted the region's proactive approach towards Web3 and digital assets since the release of its first digital asset development policy statement in 2022. According to Foresight News, the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region Government is committed to establishing a regulatory framework that balances risk management with industry growth, aiming to position Hong Kong as a global innovation hub for digital assets.

Hong Kong is making steady progress by optimizing legal and regulatory environments, expanding tokenized asset categories, promoting application scenarios, enhancing international cooperation, and developing the talent market. These efforts are designed to create a stable development environment for the digital asset industry while fostering deep integration with the real economy. Specific policy implementations include issuing licenses for stablecoin issuers, launching tokenized gold investment products, issuing digital bonds, and participating in the Project Ensemble sandbox initiative. These measures underscore Hong Kong's determination and practical actions in the Web3 and digital asset innovation sector.

data.title: STOCKS | Hong Kong Consumer Electronics Stocks Strengthen
data.description: Hong Kong's consumer electronics sector saw significant gains today, with several key players experiencing notable increases in their stock prices. According to Jin10, Lens Technology (06613.HK) led the charge with a 6.14% rise. AAC Technologies Holdings Inc. (02018.HK) followed with a 3.63% increase, while Cowell e Holdings Inc. (01415.HK) edged up by 0.85%. These movements reflect growing investor confidence in the consumer electronics market in Hong Kong.

data.title: Hong Kong Stocks: Qunhe Technology Surges Nearly 40%
data.description: Qunhe Technology (00068.HK) has seen a significant increase in its stock price, currently rising nearly 40% to 25.96 Hong Kong dollars. According to Jin10, this surge reflects strong market interest and investor confidence in the company's performance. The stock's upward trajectory highlights its potential in the Hong Kong market.

data.title: Morgan Stanley: Risks from the Iranian situation have escalated again; recommends reducing exposure to Asian and emerging market equities.
data.description: Morgan Stanley strategists say that renewed market caution stems from escalating tensions between the US and Iran over the weekend and a renewed standoff in the Strait of Hormuz. Investors who repositioned themselves in Asian and emerging market equities earlier this month should begin reducing their risk exposure. (Jinshi)

data.title: AI Tool Breach Potentially Exposes Web3 Credentials
data.description:
A recent security breach linked to a compromised AI tool may have exposed credentials used by app frontends, which serve as the user-facing layer connecting web3 wallets and trading interfaces to backend services. According to CoinDesk, this incident raises concerns about the security of digital assets and the integrity of web3 applications. The breach highlights vulnerabilities in the integration of AI tools within the web3 ecosystem, emphasizing the need for enhanced security measures to protect sensitive information.

The compromised credentials could potentially allow unauthorized access to user accounts and trading platforms, posing significant risks to both individual users and larger financial systems. As web3 applications continue to evolve, the reliance on AI tools for seamless user experiences must be balanced with robust security protocols to prevent such breaches. This incident serves as a reminder for developers and companies to prioritize cybersecurity in the development and deployment of AI-driven solutions within the web3 space. Ensuring the protection of user data and maintaining trust in digital platforms are crucial as the industry navigates the complexities of integrating advanced technologies.

data.title: Hong Kong to Formulate AI Strategy Amid $2 Billion Tokenized Bond Issuance
data.description: Hong Kong Financial Secretary Chan Mo-po announced plans to establish an AI committee and develop an industry strategy as the issuance of tokenized green and infrastructure bonds surpasses $2 billion. According to NS3.AI, Chan emphasized the potential of AI and Web3 as transformative technologies, highlighting Hong Kong's pilot of tokenized deposit applications through the Ensemble project. He also stressed the importance of balanced AI regulation that fosters innovation while ensuring human oversight and addressing cybersecurity and fraud risks.

data.title: AI TRENDS | U.S. AI Model Call Volume Surpasses China's for First Time in Two Months
data.description: According to NS3.AI, OpenRouter data indicates that the U.S. AI large model call volume reached 4.908 trillion tokens last week, surpassing China's 4.441 trillion tokens for the first time in nearly two months. China's volume experienced a decline of 23.77% from the previous week, while the U.S. volume saw an increase of 20.62%.

data.title: The weekly usage of China's large AI models dropped by 23.8% week-on-week, with the US surpassing China for the first time in nearly two months.
data.description: According to the latest data from OpenRouter, the total global AI large-scale model calls last week (April 13-19) amounted to 20.6 trillion tokens, declining for the second consecutive week. Among the listed AI large-scale models, the weekly call volume of Chinese AI large-scale models dropped to 4.441 trillion tokens, also declining for the second consecutive week, a 23.77% decrease compared to the previous week; while the weekly call volume of US AI large-scale models was 4.908 trillion tokens, a 20.62% increase week-on-week. This marks the first time in nearly two months that the weekly call volume of US AI large-scale models has surpassed that of China. Four of the top nine global AI large-scale models in terms of call volume last week were from China. Among them, DeepSeek V3.2 ranked second with a weekly call volume of 1.28 trillion tokens; MiMo-V2-Pro ranked fourth with a weekly call volume of 1.15 trillion tokens, a 90% increase compared to the previous week; two MiniMax models made the list, with MiniMax M2.5 ranking sixth with a weekly call volume of 1.05 trillion tokens; and MiniMax M2.7 ranking seventh with a weekly call volume of 0.961 trillion tokens, a 19% decrease compared to the previous week.

data.title: Fu Peng joins Hong Kong-listed company Newfire Group as Chief Economist
data.description: Fu Peng, former chief economist of Northeast Securities, has recently joined Hong Kong-listed Newfire Group as its chief economist. Newfire Group has confirmed this news.

data.title: Paul Chan: Hong Kong will establish an AI committee and formulate an industry development strategy.
data.description: In a speech at the 2026 Hong Kong Fintech Week, Hong Kong Financial Secretary Paul Chan Mo-po stated that Hong Kong will establish an AI Committee and formulate an industry development strategy. Chan pointed out that the intersection of AI and Web3 will be a game-changer, with digital asset tokenization being used by financial institutions to improve efficiency and reduce costs. Hong Kong has already issued multiple rounds of tokenized green and infrastructure bonds totaling over US$2 billion and is piloting tokenized deposit applications through the Ensemble project. Chan emphasized that Hong Kong adheres to a balanced and forward-looking regulatory system in the AI ​​field, supporting innovation while ensuring that humans remain in control and paying attention to cybersecurity and fraud risks. Currently, Hong Kong is in a phase of accelerated development of 5G and AI, striving to become a hub for the research and application of next-generation technologies.

data.title: China Maintains Loan Prime Rates for 11th Consecutive Month
data.description: The People's Bank of China (PBoC) has decided to maintain the one-year Loan Prime Rate at 3.0% and the five-year Loan Prime Rate at 3.5%, marking the 11th consecutive month without change. According to NS3.AI, the one-year rate serves as the benchmark for general loans, while the five-year rate is used as a reference for mortgages. The last adjustment to these rates occurred in May of the previous year, when the PBoC reduced each by 10 basis points.

data.title: Planet Morning News
data.description: 1. A whale that previously closed a $68.47 million long ETH position has reopened after a pullback, with a holding value of $100 million; 2. Maritime analysis firm: 35 ships turned back while exiting the Strait of Hormuz in the past 36 hours; 3. Aave saw a $10.1 billion outflow, bringing total deposits down to $35.7 billion; 4. A whale deposited $5.99 million to open a long position in crude oil; 5. A multisignature address associated with the initial RAVE allocation transferred 23 million RAVE tokens, worth $23 million, to Bitget; 6. Orca: It has rotated Vercel's potentially leaked keys and deployment credentials; the protocol and user funds were unaffected; 7. Ether.fi: The protocol's liquidity vault was unaffected by the Kelp rsETH incident; 8. Iran has designated a new shipping route in the Strait of Hormuz, prohibiting passage without permission; 9. Trump: The US Marine Corps has taken control of Iranian vessels attempting to break through the US blockade.

data.title: Hong Kong Stocks Open Higher with Hang Seng Index Up 0.17%
data.description: Hong Kong's stock market opened with gains as the Hang Seng Index rose by 0.17%. According to Jin10, the Hang Seng Tech Index also saw an increase, opening up by 0.34%. Additionally, shares of Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. Limited (CATL), listed under the ticker 03750.HK, opened with a 1.16% rise.

data.title: Lithium Prices Rise Amid Weak Demand and Supply Disruptions
data.description: On April 20, Jin10 reported that SMM battery-grade lithium carbonate prices increased by 2,000 yuan to 169,500 yuan, while lithium ore prices rose by $25 to $2,415. According to Jin10, as of April 12, 2026, over 1.67 million applications for car trade-ins have been submitted, reflecting a rise in the proportion of car consumption compared to 2025. This indicates the effectiveness of the policy but also highlights the weakness in first-time purchase consumption potential. Despite the price increase, spot transactions remain weak, with a price difference of nearly 4,000 yuan between May and September contracts. The current price rise is not driven by tight spot supply. Expectations of increased demand combined with previous supply disruptions may lead to a temporary tightening of supply and demand, supporting price increases. However, whether prices can surpass previous highs remains uncertain.

data.title: Cobo Introduces AI-Focused Crypto Wallet with Enhanced Security Features
data.description: Cobo has unveiled the Cobo Agentic Wallet, a new cryptocurrency wallet designed with a focus on artificial intelligence. According to NS3.AI, this wallet incorporates a human-agent authorization protocol and execution guardrails to enhance security. The product utilizes a Pact mechanism to define task boundaries and termination conditions, while also integrating multi-party computation to ensure signature security.

data.title: Cobo launches Agentic Wallet, a dedicated wallet product for AI Agents, ensuring agents can conduct transactions and payments autonomously and securely.
data.description: Cobo has launched Cobo Agentic Wallet (CAW), a dedicated wallet product for AI agents. Key highlights include the first self-custodied agent wallet based on MPC, the Pact human-machine authorization protocol, and a recipe-driven agent scenario. This empowers agents with autonomous execution capabilities while ensuring users always maintain clear and verifiable control. The currently available MPC mode is designed for high-risk or large-value transactions, and a custodial mode for high-frequency scenarios will be launched in the future. CAW integrates with agent frameworks such as LangChain, OpenAI Agents SDK, Claude MCP, Agno, and CrewAI, and can scalably support over 80 mainstream public chains and more than 3,000 tokens. CAW aims to transform "handing over on-chain operations to AI agents" from a high-risk experiment into a controllable, reliable, and auditable system capability. Invitation codes are currently available, and documentation, the SDK, and the recipe library will be provided simultaneously.

data.title: Ethena Extends Suspension of LayerZero OFT Cross-Chain Bridge
data.description:
Ethena has announced an extension of the suspension of the LayerZero OFT cross-chain bridge. According to BlockBeats, this decision comes as Ethena has yet to receive a satisfactory root cause analysis of the rsETH incident. 

In the meantime, Ethena has released its latest proof of reserves, verified by four independent third-party organizations: Chainlink, Chaos Labs, LlamaRisk, and Harris & Trotter. These verifications confirm that the collateralization rate of USDe remains above 100%. The results have been made publicly available on Ethena's transparency page and data dashboard. 

Ethena has committed to providing ongoing updates as the situation develops.

data.title: Reserve Monitors Kelp DAO Attack, Minimal Impact Expected
data.description:
Ethereum-based stablecoin platform Reserve is closely monitoring the recent attack on Kelp DAO's LayerZero bridge. According to ChainCatcher, Reserve's official announcement indicates that holders of Reserve DTF are unlikely to be significantly affected by the incident.

In the event of a substantial devaluation of rsETH on the mainnet, estimated between 15.5% and 18.5%, there could be potential bad debt for Aave, which might impact Aave V3's USDC collateral.

The Reserve protocol's USD3 and eUSD have RSR stakers who may act as 'first loss capital' to provide protection, though the impact is expected to be minimal. The RSR over-collateralization is deemed sufficient to cover any losses.

ETH+ and bsdETH are entirely free of rsETH collateral, posing zero risk. As a precaution, Reserve has temporarily paused the minting, rebalancing, and RSR unstaking for eUSD and USD3. Redemption functions remain operational, and users seeking RSR over-collateralization protection are advised to continue holding.

data.title: A whale that had previously taken profits on a $68.47 million long ETH position reopened after a pullback, bringing its total holdings to $100 million.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ember, a whale that previously liquidated $398 million worth of ETH long positions at high levels and made a profit of $68.47 million has re-entered the market today after the pullback. This whale currently holds 44,000 ETH long positions through two wallets, with a total holding value of $100 million and an entry price of $2,289.

data.title: STOCKS | Galaxy Securities Highlights Key Investment Opportunities in A-Share Market
data.description:
On April 20, Galaxy Securities released a report outlining strategic investment opportunities in China's A-share market. According to Jin10, the report emphasizes three main areas of focus. Firstly, it highlights the importance of technological innovation, self-sufficiency, and industry prosperity, with particular attention to sectors with high performance potential such as power equipment, energy storage, semiconductors, computing power, and communication equipment.

Secondly, the report suggests that investors should consider resource sectors benefiting from the positive year-on-year change in the Producer Price Index (PPI) and rising price levels. This includes non-ferrous metals, basic chemicals, oil and petrochemicals, building materials, and steel.

Thirdly, the report advises focusing on themes related to energy and alternative demand amid ongoing geopolitical conflicts, as well as defensive sectors like coal, coal chemicals, new energy, finance, and utilities.

Additionally, the report notes that the consumer sector is currently undervalued compared to historical levels, with certain sub-sectors offering potential for recovery. It recommends paying attention to agriculture, forestry, animal husbandry, fisheries, food and beverages, and household appliances.
